title: Ontarch agent profile
description: A shared agent operating profile authored under Workstreams/.agents/profiles/*.toml: one declaration of scope, policy, gates, and logs consumed by every agent app (Claude, Cursor, Zed, Factory, OpenCode, CLI). Ontarch policies remain the enforcement authority; a profile selects a policy via `rails` and scopes it. Canon: lg_wfos_lvl_0_agent_rails.md §13. Indexed (flattened) into registry/profiles.json by `ontarch sync`.

rails.description: The Ontarch policy id this profile selects and runs under (the enforcement authority).

rails_bin.description: Optional secondary Ontarch policy id for bin/archive mutation rails (e.g. agent-bin). Preserves the primary rails string while selecting an additional policy.

isolation.description: Where a scoped agent runs. Agents default to local branch/worktree scope (agent-rails §13.4); the main worktree is reserved. jj is opt-in for change-oriented workflows, never the default.

mode.description: worktree = run in a dedicated git worktree; branch = a scoped branch off the working repo; main = the main worktree (elevated profiles only).

jj.description: jj (Jujutsu) availability for this profile. opt-in is the sanctioned value; default/off reserved.

loads_external.description: Whether this profile may load external (third-party) agent skills. When true, required_validators MUST include skillspector_scan (enforced by ontarch validate) — an unscanned skill is treated like an unreviewed dependency and does not load.

allowed_skill_ids.description: Skill IDs this profile may invoke on demand. Bodies resolve at invocation only — never pre-loaded in agent context.

items.description: Known validator vocabulary: skillspector_scan (mandatory for any profile that loads external skills — an unscanned skill does not load), markdown_links, frontmatter, git_diff_review, format, typecheck, tests.

compressor.description: Output compressor for this profile. `rtk` wires RTK transparent command rewriting; omit or `none` to opt out (swappable).

session_log_target.description: Tracked Ontarch build-session provenance target (e.g. registry/sessions/). Never reused as the operational Takogami runtime-session state home.

runtime.description: Optional runtime-controller (Takogami) settings. Distinct from logs.session_log_target.

session_state_home.description: Optional override for operational runtime-session records. Precedence: CLI --state-home → TAKOGAMI_STATE_HOME → this field → XDG_STATE_HOME/takogami/sessions → ~/.local/state/takogami/sessions. Must not point at logs.session_log_target.
